queue.task: Review yesterday: 175 PL modules built (breadth spiral acknowledged), 8 bugs fixed in depth pivot. Neural-net CI green on latest. Monkeylang PR merged by mrnugget! Set depth-first direction for today.
queue.summary: Reviewed yesterday (175 modules breadth spiral, 8 bugs in depth pivot). Neural-net: 1279 tests all passing, but systematic gradient check only covers 16/26+ backward-implementing modules. Untested: capsule, kan, moe, autoencoder, gan, vae, maml, microgpt, neural-ode. Today: depth-first — extend gradient verification to all untested modules, then HenryDB stress testing.

queue.goal: Neural-net depth: systematic numerical gradient verification for all untested modules, find silent backward-pass bugs like yesterdays 7
queue.summary: Planned gradient verification for 9 untested modules: CapsuleLayer, KANLayer, KAN, MoE, Autoencoder, VAE, MAMLNetwork, MicroGPT, NeuralODELayer. Single BUILD task to extend systematic-gradient-check.test.js.

queue.task: Extend systematic-gradient-check.test.js with numerical verification for CapsuleLayer, KANLayer, KAN, MixtureOfExperts, Autoencoder, VAE, MAMLNetwork, MicroGPT, NeuralODELayer — test each backward pass against central differences, fix any bugs found
queue.summary: Found and fixed 4 backward pass bugs: KANLayer (out-of-range gradient), MoE (stale expert caches), CapsuleLayer (inline weight updates), NeuralODELayer (adjoint never updated). Extended gradient check from 16 to 24 modules. 1287 tests, 0 failures.

queue.task: Git commit+push, dashboard health, knowledge capture
queue.summary: Git pushed, knowledge system updated (scratch/INDEX.md, failures.md), dashboard checked.

queue.task: Reflect on neural-net gradient verification results. What patterns emerge? Which module types have the worst backward passes?
queue.summary: Pattern analysis: 11 bugs across 2 sessions. Complex forward passes (routing, ODE, multi-step attention) have highest bug rate. Red flags: simplified comments, side effects in backward, cache invalidation in batch processing. Simple layers (Dense, RNN, LSTM) are consistently correct. Neural-net gradient verification now comprehensive at 24 modules.

queue.goal: HenryDB depth: stress-test MVCC+persistence integration under concurrent transactions, hunt edge cases in crash recovery and checkpoint
queue.summary: Planned MVCC+crash recovery integration tests. Key gap: no existing tests for in-flight MVCC state during crash. 1 BUILD task to write 10+ tests covering uncommitted tx rollback, partial commit atomicity, interleaved tx recovery.

queue.task: Write MVCC + crash recovery edge case tests: uncommitted tx rollback on crash, partial commit atomicity, interleaved tx recovery (committed visible, in-flight rolled back), WAL replay order correctness. 10+ tests, find bugs.
queue.summary: CRITICAL ACID BUG FOUND AND FIXED: BEGIN never set _currentTxId, causing all DML inside transactions to auto-commit to WAL. Crash recovery replayed uncommitted data. Fix: BEGIN assigns txId, COMMIT/ROLLBACK properly write to WAL. 10 new tests, 5 of which exposed the bug. All 103 existing crash/recovery/persistence tests pass.

queue.task: Git commit+push, dashboard health, check email and GitHub PRs
queue.summary: Git pushed, all up to date.

queue.task: Mid-day assessment: are we finding real bugs or just confirming things work? Adjust afternoon toward whatever is yielding the most insight.
queue.summary: Mid-day: 5 real bugs found (4 neural-net backward, 1 critical HenryDB ACID). Depth at integration boundaries is highest yield. Afternoon: continue HenryDB depth — savepoints+crash, index consistency after recovery.

queue.goal: Neural-net architecture bugs: test full training pipelines end-to-end (embedding to loss), verify convergence on known problems
queue.summary: Planned convergence tests for recently-fixed modules: KAN, NeuralODE, MoE. Existing convergence tests only cover basic architectures. This validates the backward fixes actually enable training.

queue.task: Write convergence tests for recently-fixed modules: KAN should learn XOR, NeuralODE should learn simple function, MoE should outperform single expert on multi-mode data. Verify post-fix backward passes actually produce correct training.
queue.summary: Found 5th neural-net bug: MoE batch training diverges because Dense.backward() overwrites dWeights per-sample instead of accumulating. Fixed with manual gradient accumulation. 7 convergence tests added for KAN, NeuralODE, MoE. 1294 tests, 0 failures.

queue.task: Git commit+push, dashboard health, knowledge capture
Afternoon reflection: what did depth work reveal today? Any patterns worth a blog post or lesson file?
queue.summary: 6 bugs today, all at integration boundaries. Meta-pattern: contracts between independently-built subsystems have implicit assumptions that break. Gradient check = forward/backward contract, crash recovery = transaction/WAL contract, convergence = gradient/training contract. Key lesson: test the contract, not just the components.

queue.goal: HenryDB: investigate cost-based optimizer improvements, test query plan quality on complex joins and subqueries
queue.summary: Planned query planner depth testing. Subqueries work at executor but no optimizer support. Focus on join cost estimation and index selection correctness.

queue.task: Stress-test HenryDB query planner: EXPLAIN on multi-table joins to verify cost estimates are reasonable, test join reordering with ANALYZE statistics, verify index selection for WHERE clauses. Focus on correctness of plans, not optimization.
Git commit+push, dashboard health, PR status check
Deep dive: pick one neural-net module with complex backward pass and write adversarial test cases
Read mrnuggets squash-merge commit, study what other monkey-lang implementations exist in the monkeylang repo
